name: Vonage
one_liner: Cloud communications platform offering programmable APIs for SMS, Voice, Verify (2FA), WhatsApp/Messages, Video, and Network APIs.
primary_auth: API key + secret (Basic auth) for many APIs; JWT signed with an Application private key for Voice/Messages and advanced Verify features
access_notes: Free account signup requires no credit card and grants EUR 2 free credit plus immediate API key and secret. Trial accounts work against real APIs but are limited to the registered number plus up to 4 verified test numbers, and SMS bodies get a '[FREE SMS DEMO, TEST MESSAGE]' suffix until upgraded to a paid account.
api_notes: Extensive REST API surface across SMS, Messages, Voice, Verify, Number Insight, Numbers, Account, Video, and Network APIs, with official SDKs (Node, Python, PHP, Java, Ruby, Go, .NET) and a CLI. Easily 100+ operations across products.
mcp_notes: Vonage ships multiple official MCP servers: a Documentation MCP Server (hosted at documentation-mcp.vonage.dev/mcp) and a Tooling MCP Server that lets agents perform real SMS, voice, and WhatsApp operations; both announced on the official Vonage developer blog and published under the Vonage GitHub org.
main_blocker: None for building; live production sending requires upgrading from the trial (trial can only reach the registered/verified test numbers), so end-to-end delivery beyond test numbers needs a paid account.

evidence.claim: Vonage supports API key/secret (Basic) auth and JWT Bearer auth generated from an Application ID and private key.
Signup requires no credit card and gives EUR 2 free credit with immediate API credentials.
Trial accounts are limited to the registered number plus up to 4 verified test numbers.
Vonage officially ships MCP servers (Documentation and Tooling MCP servers) for AI agents.
Vonage exposes broad REST APIs including a public Account API reference.

open_questions: Primary auth differs per product (Basic for some Verify/SMS flows vs JWT for Voice/Messages); Composio would need per-product handling. Network APIs use a separate OAuth-style flow not fully reviewed here.
